meta_title: For Founders: freelance pitching to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Freelance Pitching to Solve Lack of SaaS Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by using freelance pitching to find paid problems. Master this advanced workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'perfect' ideas. Instead, use freelance pitching to uncover high-value pain points in niche industries. By offering to build custom AI solutions for paying clients, you validate your product-market fit before writing a single line of production code, turning market friction into recurring revenue.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re sitting on a stack of powerful AI tools, yet your mind is a void of inspiration. Most founders fail here, waiting for a lightning-bolt epiphany that never strikes. The reality is that ideas aren't found; they are harvested from the trenches of industry inefficiency.

Freelance pitching is your shortcut to market intelligence. By positioning yourself as a specialized AI architect, you force potential clients to reveal exactly what they are willing to pay for. This isn't just about building for others; it's about uncovering the exact features, workflows, and gaps that will form the foundation of your next million-dollar SaaS product.

steps.title: Niche Identification & Prospecting
steps.description: Identify a high-value industry with legacy software debt. Use your research to find specific operational bottlenecks where AI could provide immediate ROI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 5 operational pain points for boutique legal firms regarding document processing and generate a list of 10 specific, high-value automation opportunities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'research legal industry document workflow bottlenecks and summarize findings into a markdown table'

steps.title: Value-Proposition Synthesis
steps.description: Draft a pitch that focuses on outcomes, not code. You are selling the elimination of labor costs or the acceleration of revenue, not a Python script.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold outreach email that highlights the cost of manual document triage in legal firms and proposes a custom AI-agent prototype as the solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file pitch_template.md --apply 'rewrite to focus on ROI and business outcomes for partners'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Prototyping
steps.description: Once a lead expresses interest, use Antigravity to scaffold the core logic. Keep it lean to ensure you can deliver a working proof-of-concept within 48 hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust boilerplate for a document classification agent using the LangChain architecture, optimized for high-accuracy extraction from PDF invoices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'scaffold a FastAPI service that classifies and extracts data from provided PDF samples'

steps.title: Iterative Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy the prototype to a staging environment. Use Claude Code to iterate based on real-world usage patterns captured during the client's testing phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the error logs from the current agent prototype and identify the top 3 failure modes during document ingestion.

steps.title: Productization & Scaling
steps.description: If the client loves the custom tool, realize that others in the same industry have the same problem. Productize the solution into a SaaS platform.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current custom client solution into a multi-tenant SaaS architecture, abstracting the core logic into a reusable API module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor monolithic codebase into a scalable multi-tenant microservice architecture'

pro_tips: Never build a tool for free; even a nominal 'setup fee' filters out tire-kickers and validates intent.
Target 'boring' industries like logistics or compliance; they have the highest willingness to pay for AI automation.
Use Claude Code to document every edge case you encounter; these become the 'features' of your future SaaS.
If a client asks for a feature, don't build it immediately—charge for it, then build it for everyone.
Record your screen during discovery calls to feed qualitative data into your LLM for better feature prioritization.
resources: SaaS Pricing Models for AI: https://saas-school.ai
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully delivered one custom AI solution, conduct a 'Post-Mortem' analysis to identify the repeatable components. Package those into a standardized product offering and reach out to 50 similar companies to scale your revenue.
content.conversion_block: The difference between a hobbyist and a founder is the courage to ask for the check. Stop building in a vacuum and start building for the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Once a lead expresses interest, use Antigravity to scaffold the core logic. Keep it lean to ensure you can deliver a working proof-of-concept within 48 hours.
